message.content:
Research the current valuation and historical valuation for Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd, an Indian EMS/defense-electronics company (NSE: VINNY, BSE: 542907) based in Mysore.

Use WebSearch and WebFetch on screener.in (https://www.screener.in/company/VINNY/), trendlyne.com, tickertape.in, and moneycontrol.com.

Find and report with inline source URLs and the DATE the data was observed/published:
1. Current share price (note date), market cap, 52-week high and 52-week low.
2. Valuation multiples: P/E (TTM), Price-to-Book (P/B), EV/EBITDA.
3. Historical valuation range over last 2-3 years: has the P/E re-rated? Median/high/low P/E.
4. Recent revenue, net profit, EBITDA margin figures (FY2024, FY2025, any FY2026 if available), and growth rates / ROE / ROCE.

Be precise on dates for all price/valuation figures. Distinguish confirmed data from estimates. Return findings as a concise markdown section with source URLs. Do NOT write any files - return your findings directly as text.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
I have enough data to compile the report. The pocketful historical data is unreliable (showing zeros). I have a clear picture from screener, tickertape, stockanalysis, and trendlyne. Let me compile.

---

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd — Valuation Research

**Critical ticker note:** The correct NSE symbol is **VINYAS** (not VINNY). The screener.in slug `VINNY` redirects to a completely different company — *Vinny Overseas Ltd* (a ₹50 Cr textile microcap trading at ~₹1.09). The correct screener page is **https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/**. All figures below are for the right company (Mysore-based EMS/defence-electronics, ~₹1,800 Cr market cap, face value ₹10).

## 1. Current Price, Market Cap, 52-Week Range
*(Observed 15 Jun 2026)*

| Metric | Value | Source / Date |
|---|---|---|
| Share price | **₹1,404.95** (down ~3.94% on the day) | [tickertape.in](https://www.tickertape.in/stocks/vinyas-innovative-technologies-VINYA) / [trendlyne.com](https://trendlyne.com/equity/1715908/VINYAS/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/), 15 Jun 2026 3:52 PM IST |
| Market cap | **₹1,768–1,841 Cr** (Screener ₹1,768 Cr; Tickertape/stockanalysis ₹1,840 Cr) | [screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/), [stockanalysis.com](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics/), 14–15 Jun 2026 |
| 52-week high | **₹1,524** | [tickertape.in](https://www.tickertape.in/stocks/vinyas-innovative-technologies-VINYA), 12–15 Jun 2026 |
| 52-week low | **₹809** | same |
| 1-yr price change | **+~49.8%** | [stockanalysis.com](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics/) |

## 2. Valuation Multiples *(15 Jun 2026)*

| Multiple | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **P/E (TTM)** | **57–60×** (Screener 57.3; stockanalysis 60.04; Tickertape 59.62) | [screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/), [stockanalysis.com](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics/) |
| **P/B** | **~8×** (Screener 8.0; stockanalysis 7.81) — *Tickertape's 12.5 looks anomalous; treat ~8× as confirmed* | [screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/), [stockanalysis.com](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics/) |
| **EV/EBITDA** | **~32×** (stockanalysis 32.34; EV ≈ ₹1,953 Cr) | [stockanalysis.com](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics/) |
| EV/Revenue | 3.80× | same |
| Dividend yield | 0% (no dividends) | multiple |
| Book value/share | ~₹187 | [screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/) |

Note: a May 2026 search snippet cited a higher P/E (~71.7×, 3-yr avg ~80.9×) and EV/EBITDA ~21.3×; these conflict with the directly-fetched 15 Jun pages, so I treat the fetched figures (P/E ~57–60×, EV/EBITDA ~32×) as the confirmed current values.

## 3. Historical Valuation / Re-rating
Reliable per-year P/E history was not cleanly retrievable — pocketful.in returned corrupted data (all historical years showing "0") and Trendlyne's history is paywalled. What is supportable:
- The stock has **clearly re-rated upward**: 1-year price CAGR ~+49%, and stockanalysis confirms +49.8% over 52 weeks, with the stock near its 52-week high (₹1,524) vs. low (₹809) — roughly an 88% peak-to-trough spread within the year.
- Both Tickertape and stockanalysis flag the stock as **"Expensive / High Valuation."** A P/E in the high-50s on a small-cap EMS name reflects a growth-premium re-rating consistent with the defence-electronics sector re-rating across Indian small-caps over FY24–FY26.
- **Caveat (estimate, not confirmed):** one search source suggested a 3-yr average P/E ~80× and EV/EBITDA 3-yr avg ~22.7×, implying the current P/E is *below* its recent historical average. I could not independently verify this and flag it as unconfirmed.

## 4. Financials, Margins & Returns
*(Standalone, from Screener — FY2026 column shown as latest reported/TTM)*

| ₹ Cr | FY2024 | FY2025 | FY2026 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sales | 317 | 397 | 514 |
| Operating profit (EBITDA) | 33 | 41 | 61 |
| **OPM / EBITDA margin** | 11% | 10% | 12% |
| Net profit | 15 | 19 | 31 |

Source: [screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/)

- **FY2025 confirmed (search-corroborated):** Revenue ₹396.64 Cr (+25% YoY); Net profit ₹19.42 Cr (+26.5% YoY). ([screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/), moneycontrol-sourced search snippet)
- **FY2026** figures (₹514 Cr sales, ₹31 Cr PAT) — treat as latest-reported/TTM per Screener; verify against audited results as some sources may still be trailing/provisional.

**Growth & returns** ([screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/), [stockanalysis.com](https://stockanalysis.com/quote/nse/VINYAS/statistics/)):
- Sales CAGR: 3-yr ~30%, 5-yr ~20%
- Profit CAGR: 3-yr ~61%, 5-yr ~91%
- **ROE ~16.1%** (5-yr avg ~15%); **ROCE ~18.7%**
- Net profit margin ~6%; high debtor days (~161) — working-capital intensive, typical for EMS.

## Summary
Vinyas trades at a premium EMS/defence-electronics valuation — **~₹1,405/share, ~₹1,800 Cr market cap, P/E ~57–60×, P/B ~8×, EV/EBITDA ~32×** as of 15 Jun 2026. It has materially re-rated (price +~49% in 12 months, near 52-wk high), backed by genuine fundamental growth (sales +25–30% CAGR, profit growth 60%+, ROE ~16%, ROCE ~19%, EBITDA margins 10–12%). Multiple aggregators tag it "expensive." The biggest data caveats: (1) the VINNY screener slug is the wrong company, (2) clean year-by-year historical P/E was paywalled/corrupted so the re-rating is evidenced via price/return data rather than a verified P/E band, and (3) FY2026 figures appear to be latest-reported/TTM and should be checked against audited filings.
